stem_zh: Q1-A Suggested Solution -Elit To:The CEO of Eilt From:An Accountant Date:1 September 20X5 Subject:Key performance indicators,brand issues and new information system at Eilt. This report evaluates the comment on the choice of performance indicators at Eilt and issues around the introduction of non-financial indicators for product and service quality.The link between brand awareness and brand loyalty is discussed as well as an evaluation of how these concepts can be measured.Finally,the impact,risks and challenges that the larger amount of data generated by the new unified database will bring are discussed. (i) Set of current performance indicators The comment that Eilt's current set of indicators are 'backward looking with too much focus on results and not enough on determinants'can be split into three connected parts.First,the results are backward-looking in a simple sense that they make no prediction of the future lacking any forecast information.They do have an indirect indication of trend by supplying the previous year's data,however,this is not immediately clear from the report as there are no figures giving the year-on-year change in the major headings such as revenue and operating profit. Second,the current choice of indicators used in Eilt's report are all financial.This reflects the emphasis in Eilt's strategy of satisfying shareholders.However,it does not reflect whether these results are sustainable into the future as the overall objective requires.The comment that the set of indicators is about results appears accurate. Finally,in order to address the question of the sustainability of these results,the indicators need to show performance in the areas that willdetermine the future of the company (the determinants).Determinants relate to areas such as quality,flexibility, brand-building and innovation.These areas are difficult to measure directly through financial measures.The current choice of indicators has none of the non-financial performance indicators(NFPls)that would be expected to measure these determinants. The current supporting strategy of Eilt is 'utilising its well-known and respected brand name along with the procurement and selling skills of its staff to achieve growth in sales and improved profitability'.Therefore,measures of the performance of the brand name,the enhancement of procurement and selling skills would all seem appropriate and would need to be measured through NFPls. Product and service quality at Eilt Since Eilt wants to distinguish between product and service quality,a general measure of overall customer satisfaction does not seem appropriate,nor does a generic comparison with competitors(e.g.market share).Any quality measure must distinguish between the elements that relate to the product(such as quality and reliability of individual items and the range offered by Eilt)and those relating to service (such as the appearance of the shops,ease of finding products and helpfulness of staff). Therefore,a specific customer survey covering these items is required.It will provide non-financial scores and over time a trend analysis can be developed.By performing a general survey of the people of Beeland,this survey data can be compared to customers'perception of the competition.It is also possible to measure these by looking at customer complaints but again these have to be analysed into the headings of the reason for the complaint. For example,it is possible to gauge quality financially by looking at the value of returns but in order to distinguish between product and service,we would need reasons for the return as a faulty product speaks to product quality while an unsuitable product may be the result of poor advice by the sales staff. The concern over the controllability of product and service quality should distinguish between products and services.Service quality is wholly in the control of Eilt as it is their shop/staff/website that the customer interacts with.Product quality is less controllable as it is reliant on the supplier.However,Eilt has skill in procurement as noted in their strategy.It needs to use this skill to ensure that suppliers are selected that can maintain a level of product quality that Eilt's customers find acceptable. Product quality testing may be done by Eilt,but it is more likely that it will rely on the supplier to carry out most of this work. NFPls can be less reliable and more open to manipulation than financial indicators as the checks associated with financial indicators are more established.The internal checks relating money transactions and reconciliation to external sources such as bank records along with the subsequent external audit testing mean that these figures should be reliable.NFPls derive from data outside of the financial systems and so do not carry the same certainty.The interpretation of the results of such indicators is also more difficult compared to the simple numerical increase in cash/value shown in financial indicators. (ii) Brand issues Brand identity quickly conveys an image of quality and price that helps customers to identify the shops and so helps to create customer loyalty to the brand.Brand awareness is an indicator of the impression of Eilt's brand in the customers'minds. Brand loyalty results in continued visits and further purchases from the shops.Brand awareness comes first then brand loyalty follows,through positive interaction with the customer. Brand awareness Measuring brand awareness can be done to some extent through the revenue and market share of Eilt.The comparison with competitors,particularly in market share, will be important in assessing the significance of the results.In more detail,consumers can be tested by Eilt in order to see if they recall the characteristics (best quality and comfortable prices)of the brand that is associated with Eilt's marketing message and a visit to its shops. The problems in measuring brand awareness relate to the subjective nature of the answers given to questions about what is happening in customers'minds.This is a common feature of measuring non-financial performance indicators.The simple question of whether customers are aware of the brand can be tested by survey of the recognition of the brand name and logo and the recollection of the key marketing message.However,this does not say what value (good/bad or great/small)that these customers place on these messages.Different customers will have different expectations on quality and price which are the key characteristics for Eilt.They may also prioritise these characteristics differently.All of these subjective factors will lead to less certainty about the customers'view of the brand. Brand loyalty Measuring brand loyalty is more straight-forward than awareness.Loyalty can be measured through more easily quantified indicators such as customer retention and repeat purchase rates.Also,the ability of a brand to be able to push through price increases with little loss of demand is a key benefit of brand loyalty.Therefore,the elasticity of demand to price will also indicate customer loyalty.Brand loyalty can also be measured by considering the revenue volumes per customer and profit margins that Eilt enjoys and comparing these to other companies that sell similar products but without such a strong brand.This would be the premium value that the brand has generated. Brand loyalty is also seen in the marketing that loyal customers do among their friends and acquaintances.While this is much harder to measure,viewing social media reviews and interactions may give an indication of the word-of-mouth marketing performed by Eilt's own customers.Many brands offer rewards to existing customers who introduce new customers to the brand,and this can be measured when the new customer's account is set-up. (iii) Impact of Big Data at Eilt Big Data refers to the very large amounts of data that are now available through the increased use of technology and can be used to develop predictive information about customer behaviour.The creation of the new unified database will magnify this effect. The three characteristics of volume,velocity and variety in Big Data willbe addressed in turn. The large volume of data obtained is best understood by considering the relevant information gathering systems for Eilt.In stores,loyalty cards being swiped at checkouts allow the details of all products purchased,the time,amount and method of payment used.On the website and possibly also on Eilt's social media pages,in addition to the data that could be collected from store visits,every page visited,and every product examined or commented upon would be recorded.This information would require system hardware to store,search and process it. The velocity of Big Data refers to the short timescales for information developed to affect decisions.The information could be gathered in nearly real time,and this might be necessary in order to react quickly to information about customers who are considering a purchase.For example,it would be much less effective to text a customer with price-comparison information after they have left the store. The software must be present to allow the kind of data mining and predictive analysis required to perform these tasks. Big Data is 'big'due to the variety of types of information collected.Eilt could be looking at transaction data and browsing activities,as already mentioned but also,more unusual data types such as social media reactions(text and photographs)and geographical information from customer phones. This data is both structured and unstructured and these require different responses from the information recording and processing systems. Problems in implementing Systems that can record and process the volumes of data being produced can be expensive,in terms of both the hardware and software required.The costs are falling but as already noted the volumes of data available are rising.It is noted that Eilt's competitors already have similar systems.Therefore,this change will require large spending simply in order to catch-up with those competitors and then,constant on- going spending in order to pass and maintain an advantageous position. The storage of personal information (e.g.about customers)is an activity that must be carefully managed to ensure compliance with laws and regulation.Breaking these rules can be punished both in the legal sense but also in a reputational sense resulting in lost business.In particular,theft or loss of personal data can lead to civil legal action and bad publicity. The data obtained from qualitative sources(such as social media)can be imprecise or inaccurate and lead to inaccurate conclusions.Some data collected may be incorrect.This is sometimes referred to as the veracity problem.Also,in large volumes of data,some data may become out-of-date quickly especially about a customer's precise location and so constant monitoring of the database will be required to avoid this. Mark Scheme -Elit (i) 1 mark per point for the need for NFPls and the benefits they could bring to Eilt. Maximum 7 marks 1 mark per point covering NFPls for product and service quality and the reliability of NFPls. Maximum 9marks (ii) Describe brand loyalty and awareness-up to 3 marks Measurement of brand loyalty and awareness and its problems -up to 10 marks Maximum 12 marks (iii) 1 mark per point including discussions on volume,velocity and variety,and the problems of implementation. Maximum 12 marks Professional skills marks Communication: General report format and structure (use of headings/sub-headings and introduction). Style,language,and clarity (tone of report response,presentation of calculations, appropriate use of the tools) Analysis and Evaluation: Clear and systematic approach to evaluation of the friends'comment regarding NFPls. Implications of the 3Vs and implementation concerns are logically evaluated for Eilt. Scepticism: Critical discussion of the problems of measuring brand awareness and loyalty is supplied. Commercial Acumen: Discussion of possible NFPls for product quality and service quality at Eilt are practical and relevant. Suggestions of suitable measures for brand awareness and brand loyalty are practical and plausible for Eilt. Maximum 10 marks Total 50 marks

Q2-A Suggested solution-Tummel (a) Goal congruence An effective transfer pricing policy should ensure that the transfer price maximises the benefit to Tummel as a whole,for example,by maximising its overall profit rather than that of the individual divisions.As divisional directors receive profit-related pay according to the annual profit of their own divisions,they may act in a self-interested way.This was seen when Juicing took excessive risks by only buying 20%of its orange requirement in advance.This meant the Drinks division had to buy externally at a high price,which was not in Tummel's overall interest. A transfer price of full cost plus a 15%mark-up may not be in the interest of Tummel as a whole because even though it will encourage Juicing to transfer internally because it will automatically make a profit,it will not encourage Juicing to control its costs.These costs will be passed on to Drinks,together with a mark-up,which will incentivise Juicing to increase its costs and may lead to wasteful spending.The level of the external market price of orange juice will determine how willing each party is to transfer internally.Obscuring the true marginal cost of the orange juice,by using full cost and including a mark-up on costs,may lead to the buying division,Drinks,making sub-optimal decisions. Simplicity An effective transfer pricing policy should be clear,easily understood and able to be easily recorded.Currently,directors of Juicing and Drinks waste time in lengthy negotiations of the transfer price each quarter.This is of no benefit to Tummel as a whole as it simply shifts profit between divisions.Directors'time would be more usefully spent managing other important areas of the business. A transfer price of full cost plus a 15%mark-up is clear and easily understood once what is meant by full cost has been defined.It may,however,be difficult to determine a fair mark up.Even though Juicing buys most of its annual orange requirement in advance,and hence knows the price of its raw materials,the use of full cost means Drinks is unable to budget accurately as the transfer price is not known in advance. The use of standard cost instead of full cost may be a solution as the standard is set in advance,and it encourages Juicing to control its own costs. Divisional autonomy An effective transfer pricing policy will encourage divisions to act in an autonomous way.This will motivate divisional directors as they are able to improve their performance by negotiation and will allow divisional directors to make their own arrangements on buying and selling prices.Assuming a transfer price of full cost plus 15%is acceptable to both divisions,then it is an appropriate transfer price,providing both divisions continue to have the freedom to buy or sell juice to or from the external market. As divisional directors will act in a self-interested way,it may be that Tummel's head office may need to impose a transfer price to ensure that divisions act in a way that is congruent with the goals of Tummel as a whole.For example,head office may need to determine what a fair margin is,and whether it should be applied to the marginal or total costs of Juicing. Divisional performance evaluation The transfer pricing policy must be fair to both Juicing and Drinks,as the transfer price affects the profitability of each division and hence directors'profit-related pay award. An unfair transfer price will distort the evaluation of divisional performance and directors may become demotivated if their performance is unfairly affected by an inappropriate transfer price. (b) Profit-related pay The current profit-related pay scheme for directors rewards them according to the profitability of their own divisions.The scheme will,therefore,motivate them to work hard to improve the performance of their division,for example,by negotiating favourable transfer prices. Profit-related pay may help to recruit and retain staff who receive it.For directors who never receive it,its existence is likely to have no,or a negative,impact on their motivation,as can be seen by the high staff turnover in some divisions.This is particularly so when is due to circumstances outside directors'control,such as the imposition by head office of an unfair transfer pricing policy. As the profit-related pay relates to the divisions'annual profits,it is only likely to motivate directors in the relatively short term.This is not necessarily aligned with the interests of investors,such as the institutional shareholders,who will be looking for good long-term performance. Profit-related pay encourages directors to adopt goals for the benefit of their own division,which may not be in the best interests of Tummel as a whole.This was seen in the previous year where Juicing bought insufficient oranges in advance,which meant Drinks division had to pay a high price for orange juice externally.Also,many elements of profit may not be controllable at divisional level.Profit as a measure also relies on accounting estimates and can encourage short-term decision making. Share options An advantage of share options over the profit-related pay scheme is that directors' objectives are more aligned to those of the shareholders.Both groups would benefit from an increase in share price,as directors would be able to sell the shares for a higher price when the options are exercised.The directors would,however,have no downside risk if the share price fell below the option price,whereas the shareholders would.The upside risk for the directors is unlimited,and this may encourage them to take excessive risks to obtain high rewards. As the share options can be exercised in four years,they will also encourage directors to improve longer term performance.It should also encourage staff loyalty and reduce staff turnover.For some directors,this four-year period may be demotivating if they see the prospect of receiving rewards too remote in time,or if they do not intend to work in the business for so long. Setting a price for the share options may be difficult.Tummel's share price has been volatile.The current price may not reflect Tummel's long-term performance,as recent performance has been poor.Setting an option price based on the current share price may give directors excessive rewards when the options are exercised.Setting the price too high may mean the level of reward is insufficient to motivate directors to improve performance. Share options will only motivate directors if they believe their own performance will influence Tummel's share price.As there are several divisions,it is unclear how the performance of a single division affects Tummel's overall performance. The increase in the performance related element of directors'remuneration from 17% to 35%,in return for only a small increase in total expected remuneration,may cause stress to directors and demotivate them as they would be under too much pressure to perform well. Directors'personal tax positions may influence whether they prefer profit-related pay or share options. The managers are unlikely to welcome the introduction of performance related share options as this appears to replace a proportion of their current fixed salary.The influence of this group on share price is probably so remote that it will not motivate them to improve performance. Mark Scheme -Tummel (a) 1 mark per point: Criteria for effective transfer pricing policy-up to 3 marks Application to Tummel-up to 5 marks Comments on suitability of full cost plus 15%transfer price-up to 5 marks Maximum 10 marks (b) 1 mark per point: Profit-related pay scheme at Tummel-up to 7 marks Share options as a way to improve performance at Tummel-up to 7 marks Maximum 10 marks Professional skills: Analysis and evaluation: Systematic and logical approach taken to evaluating the proposed transfer pricing policy Scepticism: Questioning the impact of the proposed transfer pricing policy on the divisions of Tummel and Tummel as a whole. Commercial acumen: Benefits of introducing the new reward scheme are relevant to Tummel and consider a range of commercial issues. Maximum 5marks Total 25 marks

Q3-A Suggested Solution -Coille (a) Overhead cost comparison Total Total Cost per Hotel cost Driver driver driver Driver Total cost Unit cost Preventative machine maintenance 59,000 Maintenance 75 786.67 50 39,333 4.92 Set-ups 88,500 Set ups 300 295.00 200 59,000 7.38 Machine repairs 118,000 Repairs 10 11,800.00 8 94,400 11.80 Other overheads 29,500 Units 24,500 1.20 8000 9,633 1.20 202,366 25.30 workings: Product group information Standard Hotel Total Annual production/sales units 16,500 8,000 24,500 Units produced per production run 165 40 Number of production runs 100 200 300 Maintenance of machines(4 per run) 25 50 75 Breakdowns per production run 50 25 Number of breakdowns 2 8 10 The maintenance department overheads are currently apportioned according to production volumes and so both type of products incurred the same amount of overhead per unit,$12.04.Using activity-based costing,the overhead cost per unit for Hotel beds has gone up to $25.30,which is a very significant increase. This is because the machine set-up times,and preventative maintenance costs are driven by the number of production runs.There are 200 production runs for Hotel beds (8,000/40),compared to only 100 for Standards beds(16,500/165).Each run for Hotel beds produces only 40 units compared to 165 units for Standard beds.This may be due to the fact that these are supplied on a just in time basis for customers. The number of production runs required for Hotel beds is also contributing to the allocation of machine repair costs.The machines break down more frequently when manufacturing Hotel beds.This could be due to the fact they are made of stronger materials which put more strain on the machine. Analysis of activity-based costs in the maintenance department Preventative maintenance Preventative maintenance indirectly adds value to the customer,that is,it is something the customer will be prepared to pay for. This is because incurring costs on preventative maintenance will reduce the likelihood of machines breaking down and help ensure customers receive their orders on time.This is especially important for Hotel beds which are delivered on a just in time basis.Preventative maintenance will help improve performance in relation to customer satisfaction. Being able to accurately measure the costs relating to preventative maintenance and identifying what drives these costs,for example due to the stronger materials used in Hotel beds,means Coille can take steps to manage these cost drivers.This is a key aspect of ABM and could be achieved by using alternative materials,re-designing the product or staff training. Highlighting the relative cost of preventative maintenance in the maintenance department will enable Coille to focus on increasing preventative maintenance, compared to the cost of repairing broken machines.This could,in the longer term, improve financial performance by needing fewer resources to deal with machine breakdowns,for example. Machine setup Machine setup does not add value to the customer,but is an essential process,without which there would be no production.By using activity-based costing to identify these costs,Coille could manage them by scheduling longer production runs which reduce the number of setups required.This could be done by offering retailers trade discounts to place larger orders.Coille can accurately compare the costs of doing this to the cost savings from having fewer setups. Machine breakdown Machine breakdown is not a value-added activity and repairing broken machines is the largest single overhead cost,accounting for 40%($118k /$295k)of total overheads in the department.Coille should try and reduce or eliminate this cost,for example by benchmarking its performance against competitors.Understanding the costs of machine breakdown allows Coille to assess the benefits to performance of investing in or upgrading machinery. Other overheads A significant proportion,10%($29.5k /$295.0k),of overhead costs appears to be unrelated to any specific activity and it still be allocated based on volumes.This may be due to the difficulties in the ABM approach in identifying processes and cost drivers and allocating overheads.Further analysis of this cost is required.It may relate to idle time,which is a non value-adding activity,and this time could be spent performing more preventative maintenance. (b) From the information in Appendix 2,it appears that,due mainly to the large negative operating profit attributable to Hotel beds,the Standard product group generates 206% ($2,000,000 /$969,300)of total operating profits,even though it only forms 22% (16,500/75,050)of the number of product units sold.Only the Standard and Budget product groups make a profit and the profit of Standard beds is 77 times($2.000m/ $0.026m)that of Budget beds. In contrast,Hotel beds generate a large loss.This group accounts for 23% ($1,500,000 /$6,462,000)of total contribution but incurs a disproportionate amount of overheads at 46%($2,500,850/$5,492,700).This may be because production runs are short and orders are relatively small and frequent,compared to Budget beds for example,where there is one annual order for just one customer.There may be high design costs associated with Hotel beds. It would not be advisable to simply discontinue Hotel beds.This would not necessarily save overhead costs,such as design,administration and factory costs,which are fixed in the short term,and these would simply be reallocated to different product groups. Mattresses show an operating oss of $55,850 however this product group shows a contribution of $1,422,000.This accounts for 22%of the total contribution,however Mattresses are allocated 27%of overheads.It would not be advisable to discontinue the production of Mattresses as these are a complimentary product and without Mattresses,the sales of the other product groups could be adversely affected. Likewise,discontinuing the sale of Hotel beds may affect sales of Standard beds.A further analysis of the potential impact on other product groups is required before discontinuing any product group. Mark scheme-Coille (a) Overhead cost comparison Total Total Cost per Hotel cost Driver driver driver Driver Total cost Unit cost marks Preventative machine maintenance 59,000 Maintenance 75 786.67 50 39,333 4.92 1 Set-ups 88,500 Set ups 300 295.00 200 59,000 7.38 1 Machine repairs 118,000 Repairs 10 11,800.00 8 94,400 11.80 1 Other overheads 29,500 Units 24,500 1.20 8000 9,633 1.20 1 202,366 25.30 1 workings: Product group information Standard Hotel Total Annual production/sales units 16,500 8,000 24,500 Units produced per production run 165 40 Number of production runs 100 200 300 Maintenance of machines(4 per run) 25 50 75 Breakdowns per production run 50 25 Number of breakdowns 2 8 10 The calculation marks can be given for the total costs allocated to Hotel beds for each of the 4 categories,with the final mark being given for showing the total on a per unit basis-5 marks 1 mark per point for discussion on the comparison of the costs-up to 5 marks No marks for simply stating the numbers are bigger. 1 mark per point on analysis of activity-based costs in the maintenance department- up to 8 marks Maximum 15 (b) 1 mark per relevant point,up to 5 marks Up to 2 marks can be awarded for any relevant calculation which is useful to support the discussion Maximum 5marks Professional skills marks: Analysis and evaluation: Appropriate use of the data to support discussion and draw useful a useful comparison of the overhead costs under the two costing methods Analysis of the activity-based costs of the maintenance department draws relevant conclusions and links to performance improvement Commercial acumen: Recommendations on discontinuation are practical and plausible for Coille and are justified Maximum 5marks Total 25 marks Suggested Solution -Elit To:The CEO of Eilt From:An Accountant Date:1 September 20X5 Subject:Key performance indicators,brand issues and new information system at Eilt. This report evaluates the comment on the choice of performance indicators at Eilt and issues around the introduction of non-financial ndicators for product and service quality.The link between brand awareness and brand loyalty is discussed as well as an evaluation of how these concepts can be measured.Finally,the impact,risks and challenges that the larger amount of data generated by the new unified database will bring are discussed. (i) Set of current performance indicators The comment that Eilt's current set of indicators are 'backward looking with too much focus on results and not enough on determinants'can be split into three connected parts.First,the results are backward-looking in a simple sense that they make no prediction of the future acking any forecast information.They do have an indirect indication of trend by supplying the previous year's data,however,this is not immediately clear from the report as there are no figures giving the year-on-year change in the major headings such as revenue and operating profit. Second,the current choice of indicators used in Eilt's report are all financial.This reflects the emphasis in Eilt's strategy of satisfying shareholders.However,it does not reflect whether these results are sustainable into the future as the overall objective requires.The comment that the set of indicators is about results appears accurate. Finally,in order to address the question of the sustainability of these results,the indicators need to show performance in the areas that willdetermine the future of the company (the determinants).Determinants relate to areas such as quality,flexibility, brand-building and innovation.These areas are difficult to measure directly through financial measures.The current choice of indicators has none of the non-financial performance indicators(NFPls)that would be expected to measure these determinants. The current supporting strategy of Eilt is 'utilising its well-known and respected brand name along with the procurement and selling skills of its staff to achieve growth in sales and improved profitability'.Therefore,measures of the performance of the brand name,the enhancement of procurement and selling skills would all seem appropriate and would need to be measured through NFPls. Product and service quality at Eilt Since Eilt wants to distinguish between product and service quality,ageneral measure of overall customer satisfaction does not seem appropriate,nor does a generic comparison with competitors(e.g.market share).Any quality measure must distinguish between the elements that relate to the product(such as quality and reliability of individual items and the range offered by Eilt)and those relating to service (such as the appearance of the shops,ease of finding products and helpfulness of staff). Therefore,a specific customer survey covering these items is required.It will provide non-financial scores and over time a trend analysis can be developed.By performing a general survey of the people of Beeland,this survey data can be compared to customers'perception of the competition.It is also possible to measure these by looking at customer complaints but again these have to be analysed into the headings of the reason for the complaint. For example,it is possible to gauge quality financially by looking at the value of returns but in order to distinguish between product and service,we would need reasons for the return as a faulty product speaks to product quality while an unsuitable product may be the result of poor advice by the sales staff. The concern over the controllability of product and service quality should distinguish between products and services.Service quality is wholly in the control of Eilt as it is their shop/staff/website that the customer interacts with.Product quality is less controllable as it is reliant on the supplier.However,Eilt has skill in procurement as noted in their strategy.It needs to use this skill to ensure that suppliers are selected that can maintain a level of product quality that Eilt's customers find acceptable. Product quality testing may be done by Eilt,but it is more likely that it will rely on the supplier to carry out most of this work. NFPls can be less reliable and more open to manipulation than financial indicators as the checks associated with financial indicators are more established.The internal checks relating money transactions and reconciliation to external sources such as bank records along with the subsequent external audit testing mean that these figures should be reliable.NFPls derive from data outside of the financial systems and so do not carry the same certainty.The interpretation of the results of such indicators is also more difficult compared to the simple numerical increase in cash/value shown in financial indicators. (ii) Brand issues Brand identity quickly conveys an image of quality and price that helps customers to identify the shops and so helps to create customer loyalty to the brand.Brand awareness is an indicator of the impression of Eilt's brand in the customers'minds. Brand loyalty results in continued visits and further purchases from the shops.Brand awareness comes first then brand loyalty follows,through positive interaction with the customer. Brand awareness Measuring brand awareness can be done to some extent through the revenue and market share of Eilt.The comparison with competitors,particularly in market share, willbe important in assessing the significance of the results.In more detail,consumers can be tested by Eilt in order to see if they recall the characteristics(best quality and comfortable prices)of the brand that is associated with Eilt's marketing message and a visit to its shops. The problems in measuring brand awareness relate to the subjective nature of the answers given to questions about what is happening n customers'minds.This is a common feature of measuring non-financial performance indicators.The simple question of whether customers are aware of the brand can be tested by survey of the recognition of the brand name and logo and the recollection of the key marketing message.However,this does not say what value(good/bad or great/small)that these customers place on these messages.Different customers will have different expectations on quality and price which are the key characteristics for Eilt.They may also prioritise these characteristics differently.All of these subjective factors will lead to less certainty about the customers'view of the brand. Brand loyalty Measuring brand loyalty is more straight-forward than awareness.Loyalty can be measured through more easily quantified indicators such as customer retention and repeat purchase rates.Also,the ability of a brand to be able to push through price increases with little loss of demand is a key benefit of brand loyalty.Therefore,the elasticity of demand to price will also indicate customer loyalty.Brand loyalty can also be measured by considering the revenue volumes per customer and profit margins that Eilt enjoys and comparing these to other companies that sell similar products but without such a strong brand.This would be the premium value that the brand has generated. Brand loyalty is also seen in the marketing that loyal customers do among their friends and acquaintances.While this is much harder to measure,viewing social media reviews and interactions may give an indication of the word-of-mouth marketing performed by Eilt's own customers.Many brands offer rewards to existing customers who introduce new customers to the brand,and this can be measured when the new customer's account is set-up. (ii) Impact of Big Data at Eilt Big Data refers to the very large amounts of data that are now available through the increased use of technology and can be used to develop predictive information about customer behaviour.The creation of the new unified database will magnify this effect. The three characteristics of volume,velocity and variety in Big Data will be addressed in turn. The large volume of data obtained is best understood by considering the relevant information gathering systems for Eilt.In stores,loyalty cards being swiped at checkouts allow the details of all products purchased,the time,amount and method of payment used.On the website and possibly also on Eilt's social media pages,in addition to the data that could be collected from store visits,every page visited,and every product examined or commented upon would be recorded.This nformation would require system hardware to store,search and process it. The velocity of Big Data refers to the short timescales for information developed to affect decisions.The information could be gathered in nearly real time,and this might be necessary in order to react quickly to information about customers who are considering a purchase.For example,it would be much less effective to text a customer with price-comparison information after they have left the store. The software must be present to allow the kind of data mining and predictive analysis required to perform these tasks. Big Data is 'big'due to the variety of types of information collected.Eilt could be looking at transaction data and browsing activities,as already mentioned but also,more unusual data types such as social media reactions (text and photographs)and geographical information from customer phones. This data is both structured and unstructured and these require different responses from the information recording and processing systems. Problems in implementing Systems that can record and process the volumes of data being produced can be expensive,in terms of both the hardware and software required.The costs are falling but as already noted the volumes of data available are rising.It is noted that Eilt's competitors already have similar systems.Therefore,this change will require large spending simply in order to catch-up with those competitors and then,constant on- going spending in order to pass and maintain an advantageous position. The storage of personal information (e.g.about customers)is an activity that must be carefully managed to ensure compliance with laws and regulation.Breaking these rules can be punished both in the legal sense but also in a reputational sense resulting in lost business.In particular,theft or loss of personal data can lead to civil legal action and bad publicity. The data obtained from qualitative sources(such as social media)can be imprecise or inaccurate and lead to inaccurate conclusions.Some data collected may be incorrect.This is sometimes referred to as the veracity problem.Also,in large volumes of data,some data may become out-of-date quickly especially about a customer's precise location and so constant monitoring of the database will be required to avoid this. Maximum 10 marks Total 50 marks Suggested solution -Zeeland Schools (a)Economy Economy means obtaining inputs of an appropriate quality at the lowest possible cost.The only input given in the published data is teachers'salaries. The average salaries of $84,000 for teachers in Schools 1 and 2 would appear not to give value for money compared to the national average of $76,000.This may just reflect the fact that housing and public transport costs are much higher in the region than the national average. Teachers in School 1 work 20%(45/37·5)longer hours than the national average and 12·5%(45/40)longer than their colleagues in School2.On this basis,the average teacher salary is: School 1 $74,667(84,000×40/45) School 2 $84,000(84,000×40/40) National average $81,067(76,000×40/37·5) School 1 appears to give value for money in terms of economy,especially with high living costs in the region. The high average salary in School 2 may be due to the employment of highly paid specialist teachers,as there are only five similar schools in Zeeland. Efficiency Efficiency relates to obtaining the most outputs,at the required quality,from the resources used.The number of children per teacher is a measure of efficiency which can be calculated from the data given,though this does not take into account schools'objectives, for example,academic progress. To make a comparison,the number of teachers must be expressed as the number of full- time equivalents(FTE),typically 40 hours for full-time employees in Zeeland. FTE teachers in School 1: FTE teachers in national average results: (18×45)/40=20·25 (15×37·5)/40=14·06 Therefore,the numbers of children per FTE teacher are: School 1 School 2 National average 28·4 (576/20·25) 5·0(100/20) 34·1(480/14·06) School 1 seems to give less value for money relative to the national average,but a lower child to teacher ratio would mean smaller class sizes and may increase effectiveness.With just five children per teacher,School 2 appears to represent very poor value for money. This is probably due to the specialist nature of the school,where small class sizes may be needed for children who struggle to thrive and succeed in a traditional school environment. Effectiveness Effectiveness relates to whether the schools’objectives have been achieved.The published data focuses on children's academic progress,but this will not be the only objective or even the most important one. Academic progress at the two schools is: School 1 8·3%(91-84/84) School 2 10·0%(33-30/30) National average 12·5%(90-80/80) Though School 1 has the highest level of achievement,children's academic progress is lower than the national average,as is that of School 2.Neither school gives good value for money in this respect,though the objectives of School 2 mean that these results are not really comparable with those of School 1. Conclusion School 1 gives good economy,but not efficiency or effectiveness.This may be due to the employment of less well qualified,experienced and skilled teachers who are paid less. Teachers may simply be de-motivated as they are paid less than their colleagues in other schools.High efficiency will mean larger class sizes which may reduce effectiveness. School 2 appears to perform poorly in all of the 3Es compared the national average.Due to its specialist nature and probably differing objectives from most schools,it cannot be fairly compared with the national average.It is impossible to conclude whether it gives good value for money. (b) Problems in producing league tables Basis of measurement The actual level of attainment for each child is objectively measured by performance in examinations sat by all 16-year-olds in Zeeland.This can be fairly compared across all schools.The expected attainment,however,is estimated by teachers when a child enters the school,using records obtained from the child's previous school.Teachers'estimates may be unreliable,as may the previous school's records.These estimates may also be manipulated by teachers in order to make the school's performance appear better than it really is. Determination of numerical rank in the league table The use of a numerical ranking,to enable comparison of schools according to their performance in the measures published in the league table,is an attempt at simplifying comparisons between schools.This may be too simplistic,however,in that it does not take into account differences in different schools'objectives,differences between children’s' needs,or even geographical location. The determination of weightings for the different measures,according to the relative importance assigned to each measure by the ZSD,is subjective and may confuse parents. Parents may not share the ZSD's interpretation of what is important.They will place greater importance of the progress and wellbeing of their child over the value for money which each school is giving.This is a potential conflict between the two objectives of producing the league tables in the first place. Cost Around 1,900 school inspectors and other staff would need to be recruited in order to publish the fullleague tables.This will be very costly,and this money may be better spent on allocating more funding directly to the schools. Problems arising as a result of publishing league tables Pressure to achieve targets The publication of league tables creates pressure for schools to achieve academic progress.This is especially so since head teachers from schools which underperform against the national average may be removed from their posts and school funding may be reduced. By definition,some schools will always perform below the national average.To sanction them just because of this may unfairly penalise them when they may be achieving good academic achievement by other definitions.Reducing their funding may further their reduce performance,for example,there will be less funding to employ the best teachers. The pressure on teachers caused by the publication of league tables may de-motivate them,or lead to work-related stress.This may be the case in School 1 where academic achievement is well below the national average.In School 1,teachers work much longer hours than teachers in other schools,possibly because they are under pressure from the head teacher to improve the school's performance in the league tables.There is a high rate of turnover of teaching staff in School 1,which may indicate that teachers are unhappy at work,possibly due to working long hours. A poor performance in league tables may also reduce children's motivation to work hard, or stigmatise the school.It is unclear if schools in Zeeland can select which children can attend.If so,league tables would encourage the most able children to attend the best schools.This would tend to improve performance in these schools and reduce performance in the worst ones.The effect of this on overall standards is unclear. Lack of focus on other key objectives The ZSD league tables focus on academic progress,and schools may focus on this at the expense of other important areas of school life.The fixed funding available may encourage head teachers to allocate too many resources to achieving short-term academic performance.Too few resources may be allocated to longer term performance improvements or investing in recreational facilities,good school meals and a comfortable environment.The satisfaction survey indicates that parents and their children value these other aspects highly.As they focus on academic performance,the league tables may not help parents choose a school where this is not their only or main objective for their child. Zeeland Schools-Marking guide (a) Evaluation of value for money in Schools 1 and 2-1 mark per point Economy-up to 5 marks Efficiency-up to 5 marks Effectiveness-up to 5 marks Maximum 12 marks (b) 1 mark per relevant point Maximum 8marks Professional marks: Analysis and Evaluation: · Evaluation of value for money is provided and supported with appropriate use of the data · Recognition that the number of teachers must be expressed as the number of FTE for a valid comparison Scepticism: ·Recognition of the challenges in evaluating School 2's performance based on its specialist nature Commercial Acumen: · Advice on the problems of producing and publishing league table information is applied appropriately to Zeeland Schools Maximum 5marks Total 25 marks Luffield -Suggested Solution (a) Calculation of net operating profit after tax(NOPAT) Profit before interest and tax Add back: Accounting depreciation Non-cash expenses Advertising Deduct: Economic depreciation Tax charge Tax relief on interest NOPAT Capital employed at the start of the period (No adjustments required) Calculation of EVATM($m) NOPAT Capital charge (WACCxadjusted capital) EVATM Blue ($m) 24·0 10·0 一 2·0 (9 ·0) (1 ·8) (1 ·3) 23·9 500·0 23·9 (55 ·0) (31 · 1) Green $m) 31.2 5·25·0 5·2 一 (4 ·0) (1 ·6) (1 ·3) 34.5 292·0 34.5 (32 · 1) 2.4 Shareholder value of $2.4m has been created in Green during the period,and shareholder value of $31 ·1m has been destroyed in Blue.This indicates that Luffield should either seek to dispose of Blue or take action to increase shareholder value. EVATM compared to ROCE EVATM is a measure which can be used at all levels in the organisation in order to achieve the objective of maximising shareholder wealth.EVATM measures how much value is created by managers and is similar to and consistent with the concept of the net present value approach to appraising investments.Increasing EVATM will create value for shareholders and be consistent with Luffield's stated objective to maximise shareholder wealth. ROCE is a profit-based measure and does not directly correlate with shareholder value.As a result,this may lead to managers taking decisions which benefit them in the short term at the expense of long-term shareholder wealth. Expenditure which is incurred for long-term benefit,such as advertising or research expenditure,is capitalised using the EVATM approach.This will discourage managers from making short-term decisions such as reducing advertising expenditure which may be required to support the long-term development of the Luffield brand. The managers at Blue chose not to update the old storage equipment in order that costs would not hit their operating profit and decrease ROCE on which divisional managers'performance is assessed.Use of EVATM will,in the long term,help prevent managers making dysfunctional decisions such as this,which led to problems with food safety,and adverse publicity. (b) In a value-based management approach,value is determined by an organisation's future discounted cash flows and value is only created when investment decisions are made which provide long-term returns which exceed the cost of capital.VBM should be used by an organisation to help make both strategic and operational decisions. VBM involves ensuring that Luffield's overall objectives,strategies and management processes are aligned so that managers focus on creating value for the shareholders. At the whole business level,Luffield's strategy of acquiring and selling businesses should increase the overall value of the company,which can be measured using EVATM.Also at the whole business level,Luffield could attempt to increase shareholder value by considering other value drivers.These could include reducing the effective tax rate,extending the growth period or length of projects,reducing the investments in capital expenditure and working capital,and taking steps to reduce the overall cost of capital. Luffield will need to set goals in terms of future discounted cash flows and ensure these are translated into appropriate shorter-term targets.Having established the maximisation of shareholder value as a strategic objective,objectives at the business unit or divisional level should be created and performance targets should be defined. This will be done by focusing on activities which create value,known as value drivers. These drivers should be carefully defined so that responsibility can be assigned to individual divisions could be set targets for increasing revenues and profit margins.Itthose who have control over them.This will be translated into the divisions as individual divisions could be set targets for increasing revenues and profit margins.It could also be recognised that these targets might be different in the divisions could also be recognised that these targets might be different in the divisions depending on their activities,markets,capacity and so forth.Luffield will also need to introduce non-financial objectives,such as customer satisfaction,as these create long-term value in the business. Once these targets for the divisions have been determined,the next steps are to develop budgets and plans to achieve those targets.This will be linked to performance measurement and incentive schemes to encourage staff to meet their targets.The managers responsible for the targets should be appraised and rewarded according to improvements in these important value drivers.This will help to avoid the shorter-term dysfunctional decision-making which can occur,as seen in Blue. To motivate managers to achieve these targets,rewards should be linked to both the short-term and long-term targets for the key value drivers which are controllable by them. Assessment of VBM at Luffield addresses its use across all levels of the organisation and considers a number of implications. Scepticism Effective challenge of ROCE as a divisional performance measure compared to EVATM is supplied. Q1 一、题干解析 Company information Folt Manufacturing(Folt)is a company,which is majority owned by its management team.A number of years ago,it was bought out of a larger multinational business by the management team.The management were supported by a venture capital firm,who provided the remaining equity and now supply all the debt required by the business at market rates.The business manufactures and sells digital imaging devices for use in a variety of industrial situations.A key component in its products is the image processing software which is included.Folt has a small team of software developers who are beginning to gain a reputation for innovative solutions. 福尔特制造(Folt)是一家由其管理团队拥有多数股权的公司。几年前，它被管理团队从 一家更大的跨国企业中收购。这家公司的管理层得到了一家风险投资公司的支持，这 家公司提供了剩余的股权，现在以市场利率提供了公司所需的所有债务。 该公司生产 和销售用于各种工业场合的数字成像设备。其产品的一个关键组成部分是包含的图像 处理软件。Folt 有一个小的软件开发团队，他们创新的解决方案开始获得声誉。 Folt has faced increasing pressure in its home market of Beeland from manufacturers in countries with lower cost bases.As a result,it has decided to invest in developing its image processing software rather than compete on the manufacturing of the hardware (the imaging devices).It willcontinue to sell the imaging devices but the manufacturing willbe outsourced. Folt 在其本土市场Beeland 面临着来自成本基础较低国家制造商越来越大的压力。因此， 它决定投资开发自己的图像处理软件，而不是在硬件(成像设备)的制造上竞争。它将 继续销售成像设备，但制造将被外包。 Folt's overall objective is unchanged and it is 'to provide an adequate return to its capital providers while growing the business into a world-class supplier in its areas of expertise'.The chief executive officer(CEO)has identified three factors which are critical success factors (CSFs)to achieving this objective.These are: 1.Keep capital providers satisfied; 2.Build a world-class software development team;and 3.Ensure that quality of the imaging devices meets market standards. Folt 的总体目标没有改变，即“为资本提供者提供足够的回报，同时将业务发展成为 其专业领域的世界级供应商”。首席执行官(CEO)确定了实现这一目标的三个关键成功 因素(csf) 。这些是： 1.让资本提供者满意； 2.打造世界一流的软件开发团队；和 3.确保成像设备的质量符合市场标准。 Performance measurement system The CEO requires assistance from a performance management expert to create an appropriate performance measurement system for Folt.First,he needs recommendations of suitably justified key performance indicators(KPIs)for each CSF.The CEO has indicated that there should be a maximum of two KPIs per CSF in order to avoid information overload.Then,he wants an assessment of the use of this group of KPIs to measure the strategic performance of Folt. 首席执行官需要一位绩效管理专家协助，为 Folt 创建一个适当的绩效评估系统。首先， 他需要为每个CSF 提供适当的关键绩效指标(KPI)建议。首席执行官表示，每个CSF 最 多有两个 KPI, 以避免信息过载。然后，他希望对这组 KPI 的使用情况进行评估，以 衡量 Folt 的战略绩效。 Outsourced manufacturing:Initial plans The company has selected a suitable manufacturer for the outsourced work.Xela Manufacturing (Xela)is based in Ceeland,which is a much lower cost environment than Beeland.Folt is preparing to enter into detailed contract negotiations with Xela. 该公司已为这项外包工作选择了一家合适的制造商。 Xela 制造商 (Xela) 总部位于 Ceeland,比 Beeland 低得多的成本环境中。 Folt正准备与Xela 进行详细的合同谈判。 The manufacturing will be done under licence from Folt.This means that Folt supplies the product designs but retains the intellectual rights to them and Xela manufactures to agreed standards of quality.Xela then despatches the devices back to Folt who add the software, package the product and send it to their customers.The quality standards will be set by the service level agreements (SLAs)which will be agreed in the contract. 生产将在 Folt 的许可下进行。这意味着 Folt 提供产品设计，但保留其知识产权， Xela 按照商定的质量标准生产。然后 Xela 将设备发送回 Folt, 后者添加软件，包装产品并 将其发送给客户。质量标准将由合同中商定的服务水平协议(SLA) 设定。 In preparation for these negotiations,the CEO needs advice on the following three critical areas (target costing,responsibility for quality areas and sources of information)for the negotiation of the agreement. 在准备这些谈判时，首席执行官需要在以下三个关键领域(目标成本计算、质量领域的 责任和信息来源)上为协议谈判提供建议。 Target costing The CEO realises that he needs to understand the cost structure of the products in order to have a firm basis for price negotiation with Xela.He has heard that target costing could be helpful. Therefore,he needs to knowhow target costing works and how it might be used in this situation. In order to clarify the explanation,he has gathered information(Appendix 1)on one product (Product 123)which requires a redesign before being relaunched into the market.He wants an example calculation of the target cost gap and an explanation of how the result of this would impact on negotiations with Xela. CEO 意识到他需要了解产品的成本结构，以便与Xela 进行价格谈判有一个坚实的基础。 他听说目标成本核算可能会有所帮助。因此，他需要知道目标成本是如何工作的，以 及如何在这种情况下使用它。为了解释，他收集了一个产品(产品123)的信息(附录1), 该产品在重新投放市场之前需要重新设计。他想要一个计算目标成本差距的例子，并 解释其结果将如何影响与Xela的谈判。 Responsibility for quality areas The maintenance ofexisting quality is a critical concern for Folt,since it will focus on software as the key selling point but this will not be sufficient for market success if the hardware does not meet market standards.The CEO wants to understand,in terms of the four areas of quality costs,how responsibility for each area should be attributed between Folt and Xela. 对 Folt 来说，维持现有质量是一个关键问题，因为它将把重点放在软件上作为关键卖 点，但如果硬件不符合市场标准，这将不足以取得市场成功。CEO 想要了解，就四个 质量成本领域而言，每个领域的责任应该如何在Folt 和 Xela 之间分配。 Sources of information Folt has appropriate information systems in place for its existing manufacturing operation.The CEO and board are happy with their operation at present.However,Xela has indicated that its current information systems are purely financial,aiming to collect the required information for its financial reporting duties.The CEO is worried about this and wants an assessment of the impact of this on the management of quality within the contract. Folt 已为其现有的制造业务建立了适当的信息系统。首席执行官和董事会对他们目前 的运作很满意。然而，Xela 表示，其目前的信息系统纯粹是财务方面的，旨在收集其 财务报告职责所需的信息。首席执行官对此很担心，并希望对合同中质量管理的影响 进行评估。 Appendix 1 Information on Product 123 The company aims for a profit margin of 20%on Product 123.Target price is $175 per unit, based on sales and marketing department research. Cost details: 1.The remaining commercial life of the product is two years. 2.It is estimated that 250,000 units will be sold over the remaining life of the product. 3.Materials cost$37 per unit. 4.Each unit will take 0·5 hours oflabour at an average cost of $25 per hour. 5.Each unit will use 11 hours of machine time at an average cost(including overheads)of $32 per hour. 6.Packaging and delivery will cost $8 per unit. 7.The design costs of the unit are expected to total $2m. 8.Inspections cost $100,000 per annum-the redesign will not affect this. 9.The rate of failed products,either spotted by customers or by the inspection team,is expected to remain at 2·5%. 10.Failed products will be reworked at an average cost of $20 per unit. 11.The software package supplied by Folt costs $40 per unit. 产品123的信息： 公司的目标是123号产品的利润率达到20%。根据销售和市场部门的研究，目标价格 是每台175美元。 具体成本信息：
10. 不合格的产品将以平均每件20美元的成本进行返工。 11.Folt提供的软件包每套售价40美元。 二、问题
explanation_zh: Required: It is now 1 September 20X5. Write a report to the CEO of Folt Manufacturing(Folt)to: (i)Recommend the key performance indicators and evaluate them as required by the chief executive officer(CEO). (16 marks) (ii)Respond to the CEO's request for work on target costing. (10 marks) (iii)Explain the four quality cost areas and evaluate how to divide responsibilities for these under the outsourcing contract. (11 marks) (iv)Assess the impact of the sources of information on the management of quality within the outsourcing contract. (9 marks) Professional marks willbe awarded for the format,style and structure of the discussion of your answer. (4 marks) (50 marks) 现在是20X5 年9月1日。 向 Folt Manufacturing(Folt)的 CEO 写一份报告： (i) 建议关键业绩指标，并按CEO 的要求对其进行评价。 (16分) (ii) 响应CEO 关于目标成本核算工作的要求。 (10分) iii) 解释四个质量成本领域，并评估在外包合同下如何划分这些领域的责任。(11分) (iv) 评估信息来源对外包合同质量管理的影响。 (9分) 讨论答案的形式、风格和结构将获得专业分数。 (4分) 高顿教育 GOLDEN EDUCATION 内部资料，仅供学习交流使用 三、答题思路： 这道题目是需要写一份报告，所以需要拥有报告的基础格式 Report heading 共四行，四要素中缺少一个不得分，四要素顺序错误不得分 To: 接收对象+公司名 标准：正确的接收对象，可无公司名称 From: 指定角色/Management accountant /A accountant 标准：除文中指定角色，其它均可 Date:指定日期/考试日期 标准：指定日期优先 Subject: 根据 section A中第一个问题内容一般分为以下2种情况 评估Performance report——Performance reporting+management issues+ 公司名 其它知识点——Performance measurement and management+公司名 标准：除以上原则外，可总结section A中问题的知识点(可参考2019 M/J) Introduction 能够运用连接词描述Section A中所有问题；缺少一个问题描述不得分。 PLATINUM To:The CEO of Arkaig Manufacturing(Arkaig) From:An Accountant Date:September 20X5 Subject:Performance measurement and outsourcing of manufacturing at Folt This report recommends a set of key performance indicators(KPIs)for the identified critical success factors(CSFs)associated with the new strategy and evaluates these as a set of measures of the strategic performance of Folt.Further,in preparation for the negotiation of the outsourced manufacturing contract,advice is given on the use of target costing;the attribution of responsibility for areas of quality and the information required to monitor the quality of production. 本报告针对已确定的与新战略相关的关键成功因素提出了一套关键绩效指标(KPI), 并 将其作为衡量 Folt 战略绩效的一套措施进行评估。此外，在准备外包制造合同的谈判 时，就目标成本的使用提出建议；质量领域的责任归属和监控生产质量所需的信息。 (i)Recommended KPIs for CSFs 根据原文中要求： 一是 “First,he needs recommendations of suitably justified key performance indicators (KPIs)for each CSF.The CEO has indicated that there should be a maximum of two KPIs per CSF in order to avoid information overload.”首先，他需要为每个CSF 提供适当的 关键绩效指标(KPI)建议。每个CSF 最多有两个KPI, 以避免信息过载。可以三个CSF 为框架来回答。 建议关键绩效指标-每个CSF 最多4分，必须在答案中证明是合理的 每个 CSF 最多建议2个KPI-1 分 1.Keep capital providers satisfied; √ 分 析 现 状 · 有两个资本提供者，债务全部由风险投资家(VC) 提供，股权由管理团队提供， 而 VC 与管理团队合并占多数。 There are two capital providers,the debt is all provided by the venture capitalist(VC) and the equity is provided by the management team and the VC combined with the management team taking the majority. √ 建 议KPI: ■ return on capital employed(ROCE)which measures the returns to all capital providers as a group. possibly by using an industry average as benchmark. ·To break this down further would probably require return on equity and cost of debt 2.Build a world-class software development team; √ 分析现状： ■ 这是一个很难精确衡量的因素建立团队可以通过增加员工数量或承担的项目数 量来衡量 This is a difficult factor to measure with precision.Building a team could be measured by an increasing number of employees or number of projects undertaken ■ 但这个CSF 的重要元素是“世界级”的团队素质。 the important element in this CSF is the ‘world-class'quality of the team. · 这可以用团队的投入成本来衡量，只要他们是按有竞争力的市场价格招聘的， 因为工资越高意味着技能越高。 This could be measured by the input cost of the team,provided that they are recruited at competitive market rates,since higher wages would imply higher skills. · 可以用团队的商业成就(销售额)或技术成就(行业设计奖)来衡量 it could be measured by the team's output in terms of commercial success (sales)or technical achievement(industry design awards). √ 建 议KPI: ·by the output of the team,it is recommended that we use growth in number of projects undertaken and the number of industry design awards won as the KPIs for this CSF. 3.Ensure that quality of the imaging devices meets market standards. √ 分析现状： · 通过观察对销量和盈利能力的影响来衡量质量的财务指标(质量越高，两者可能 越高) Financial measures of quality are possible by looking at the effect on sales volume and profitability(both likely higher for higher quality). · 然而，这些都是滞后的指标，质量的影响需要相当长的时间才能逐渐体现在顾 客的感受上。 However,these are lagging indicators where quality effects can take some considerable time to trickle through to customer perceptions. ■ 衡量当前制造业质量的一个更直接的指标是顾客退货率和工厂测试的产品不良 率 A more direct measure of the quality of current manufacturing would be through customer returns and product failure Q2 一 、题干解析 1.Vunderg:Company information Vunderg manufactures windows and related products.The overheads in the business are high and primarily relate to procurement,customer administration and product design.Procurement and customer administration overheads relate to all of Vunderg’s product groups.Design overheads currently relate to only two product groups,glasshouses and conservatories. Vunderg's current costing system has been in use for a long time and absorbs overheads into product costs on a direct labour hours basis.The current costing system does not involve identifying the factors which drive the costs of the company's activities and allocating overheads to products based on their usage of those factors. Vunderg生产窗户及相关产品。该公司的间接费用很高，主要涉及采购、客户管理和产 品设计。采购和客户管理间接费用与 Vunderg 的所有产品有关。目前，设计间接费用 只与两组产品：玻璃暖房和温室有关。Vunderg 目前的成本核算系统已经使用了很长时 间，并以直接人工小时为基础将间接费用吸收到产品成本中。现行成本核算制度并不 涉及识别驱动公司活动的成本因素，并根据产品对这些因素的使用情况分配间接费用。 2.Current product range GOLDEN EDUCATION ONLINE DELIVERY-PLATINUM Windows For many years,Vunderg has manufactured UPVC window frames used for residential house building.Vunderg buys the glass from an external supplier,inserts it into a frame and sells the completed window units to a small number of large construction companies.These window units all have standard designs,specifications and sizes.This market is very competitive,and Vunderg must offer a complete range of windows for sale to the construction companies. 窗户 多年来，Vunderg 一直生产用于住宅的 UPVC non-commercial use,made of attractive and high quality materials.After initial quality problems due to the use 高顿教育 GOLDEN EDUCATION 内部资料，仅供学习交流使用 of materials and manufacturing methods new to size of orders they place and the level of customer support and trade discounts which they receive from Vunderg.As a result,the profitability of selling to different retailers,or types of retailer,varies significantly. 玻璃暖房 五年前，Vunderg 使用吸引人的高质量材料，开发了一系列非商业用途的小型花园玻璃 暖房。因为 Vunderg 使用了新材料和制造方法，在产生初期出现了一些质量问题。而 现在玻璃暖房生产是高效的，并且质量很高，因此目前售价很高。这些玻璃暖房通过 40家零售商在全国范围内销售，这些零售商在订单规模、客户支持水平和从
